characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The main character, who trudges through life with his dull corporate job and countless group meetings until he meets Tyler Durden. Ambiguously Bi: While he's sexually attracted to Marla,his alter ego Tyler inFight Club 2has no issue sleeping with men. However, when Tyler's male suitor Angel Face kisses the Narrator, the latter is clearly unhappy with the kiss. The Anti-Nihilist: In the novel, while the Narrator agrees with Tyler that no one is a special snowflake, he still values human life and isn't fine with Tyler's terrorism. Ate His Gun:How he defeats Tyler Durden, although he survives since he shot himself through the cheek. Beware the Quiet Ones: Seems calm and morose, but as Angel Face can attest, he can beviciousin a fight.

characters.name: "Jack"/The Narrator
characters.description: The main character, who trudges through life with his dull corporate job and countless group meetings until he meets Tyler Durden. Ambiguously Bi: While he's sexually attracted to Marla,his alter ego Tyler inFight Club 2has no issue sleeping with men. However, when Tyler's male suitor Angel Face kisses the Narrator, the latter is clearly unhappy with the kiss. The Anti-Nihilist: In the novel, while the Narrator agrees with Tyler that no one is a special snowflake, he still values human life and isn't fine with Tyler's terrorism. Beware the Quiet Ones: Seems calm and morose, but as Angel Face can attest, he can beviciousin a fight.

characters.name: Tyler Durden
characters.description: A mysterious man who makes friends with the Narrator. He holds anarchist and anti-consumerism beliefs, and he takes to organizing the titular Fight Club. He eventually descends into outright terrorism with a group of his own, Project Mayhem. Adaptational Heroism:Downplayedin the film. Tyler's master plan might be as destructive as in the book, and arguably even more so, as the film sees himtargeting multiple buildings, unlike in the book where he only targets one building. But also unlike in the book, he makes sure that the buildings he plans to blow up are emptied of people beforehand. In addition, in the film, he's targeting office buildings belonging to the world's leading credit companies while in the book, he's trying to blow up amuseum, something people are less likely to support. Adaptation Dye-Job:Downplayed. In the novel, Tyler is described as having blond hair, but the film version has dark-blond hair and can be mistaken for a brunet. The comics, which take place in the novel's continuity, depict Tyler with bright-blond hair. Anthropomorphic Personification:Tyler is the narrator's frustration at his powerless, meaningless existence embodied into his image of what a "real man" looks and acts like. It's important that he first appears when the narrator loses his coping mechanism of support groups — with no outlet for his emotions, his psyche cracks and pours all of his frustrations into Tyler. Ax-Crazy: He is violent, unpredictable, sadistic, very open to murder, aMad Bomber, commits brutal acts randomly, and is generally aStraw Nihilistwho lives a life of pure hedonism.

characters.name: Marla Singer
characters.description: A headstrong woman the Narrator first meets in a support group. He initially dislikes her for interfering with his method of dealing with insomnia, but eventually the two work up an uneasy rapport. Cloudcuckoolander: Comes off as more than a bit strange, but mellows out a bit later on in the movie. Driven to Suicide: Subverted. She swallows a whole bottle of Xanax, but survives. She admits it was "probably one of those cry-for-help things." Eerie Pale-Skinned Brunette: Well, sheisplayed byHelena Bonham Carter. Establishing Character Moment: Loudly walks into a support group while smoking, during a sensitive moment, and asks if it's the cancer group. The fact that she's smoking during this scene immediately paints her as callous, while the fact that it's a support group for testicular cancer survivors totally goes over her head.

characters.name: Robert Paulson
characters.description: An overweight man with gynecomastia who the Narrator meets at a support group for survivors of testicular cancer. He eventually joins Fight Club, and later Project Mayhem. Beauty to Beast: His backstory. He used to be a proud, vain bodybuilder, only for the steroids he was using to give him testicular cancer, which led to hormonal changes that not only robbed him of his physique but also caused him to develop large breasts in a grotesque mockery of the sort of hypermasculinity he once pursued. Boom, Headshot!:He's killed when a security officer shoots him in the back of the head. Irony: Bob's use of steroids in pursuit of an ideal masculine physique led to him getting testicular cancer that required his testicles to be removed, As a result, he needs to take hormones that left his body flabby and with feminized breasts. What's more, the fitness program he developed and marketed on late-night TV is described as having been a chest-expansion program, and losing his testicles ultimately led to avery different kind of "chest expansion". I Was Quite a Looker: He used to be a bodybuilder who took steroids. He stopped doing both after getting testicular cancer,and it shows.

characters.name: Angel Face
characters.description: A regular at Fight Club. After Tyler shows a fondness for him, the Narrator beats his face into a pulp with the explanation that he "wanted to destroy something beautiful." Beauty to Beast:Invoked. The Narrator gives Angel Face aNo-Holds-Barred Beatdownwith the specific intention of disfiguring him, as the former wanted to "destroy something beautiful." Eye Scream: One of his eyes is completely swollen and covered over by skin after he gets beaten. Facial Horror: His face after getting brutally beaten by the Narrator is less than pretty, to put it mildly. Manly Gay: InFight Club 2, Angel Face has kept up his fight sessions in the eponymous organization, maintaining a muscular, scarred physique. He also falls in love with Tyler.
